channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: An Israeli strike in Gaza kills World Central Kitchen workers. Israel says 1 was an Oct. 7 attacker
item.description: DEIR AL-BALAH, Gaza Strip (AP) — An Israeli airstrike on a car in the Gaza Strip on Saturday killed five people, including employees of World Central Kitchen.
item.dc:creator: WAFAA SHURAFA, TIA GOLDENBERG and BASSEM MROUE, Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: Truck carrying 44,000 pounds of dried chickpeas catches fire in Death Valley National Park
item.description: A truck carrying 44,000 pounds of dried chickpeas caught fire in Death Valley National Park, according to the National Park Service.
item.dc:creator: Linsey Lewis, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Earl Holliman, 'The Rainmaker' and 'Twilight Zone' actor, dead at 96
item.description: (NEXSTAR) - Award-winning actor Earl Holliman has died at the age of 96, according to multiple reports. Holliman died Monday in hospice care at his home in Studio City according to his spouse, Craig Curtis, who told The Hollywood Reporter.
item.dc:creator: Nick Jachim, ABC 4

item.title: Taiwan's president arrives in Hawaii for a 2-day stop in the US as part of South Pacific visit
item.description: HONOLULU (AP) — Taiwan's president Lai Ching-te arrived Saturday in Hawaii to begin a two-day transit in the U.S. as part of a trip to the South Pacific, his first since assuming office.
item.dc:creator: TAIJING WU, HUIZHONG WU and AUDREY McAVOY, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Bob Bryar, former My Chemical Romance drummer, dies at 44
item.description: (WGHP) — Bob Bryar, the drummer of the rock band My Chemical Romance from 2004 to 2014 has died, TMZ reports. He was 44. According to TMZ, Bryar was found dead in his Tennessee home Tuesday after last being seen alive on November 4.
item.dc:creator: Michaela Ratliff, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Missing hiker survived 50 days in Canadian wilderness, at times in frigid temps
item.description: A 20-year-old hiker who had been missing for weeks was found alive in the wilderness of British Columbia, the Royal Canadian Mountain Police confirmed on Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: Jordan Unger, ABC 4

item.title: Iceland votes for a new parliament after political disagreements force an early election
item.description: REYKJAVIK, Iceland (AP) — Icelanders voted to elect a new parliament Saturday after disagreements over immigration, energy policy and the economy forced Prime Minister Bjarni Benediktsson to pull the plug on his coalition government and call an early election.
item.dc:creator: MARCO DI MARCO and DANICA KIRKA, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Southwest Airlines says it is ending cabin service earlier to reduce chance of injury
item.description: Southwest Airlines says it is ending its cabin service earlier on its flights starting next month. Beginning on Dec. 4, a company spokesperson said, flight attendants will begin preparing the cabin for landing at an altitude of 18,000 feet (5,486 meters) instead of 10,000 feet (3,048 meters).
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Credit card debt is soaring – what to know about getting out of it
item.description: With credit card debt reaching an all-time high, now is the time for the most pertinent questions and answers surrounding a difficult reality many Americans are facing.
item.dc:creator: Patrick Djordjevic, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Canadian Prime Minister Trudeau flies to Florida to meet with Trump after tariffs threat
item.description: WEST PALM BEACH, Fla. (AP) — Canadian Prime Minister Justin Trudeau flew to Florida on Friday to have dinner with President-elect Donald Trump at his Mar-a-Lago club after Trump threatened to impose sweeping tariffs on Canadian products.
item.dc:creator: ROB GILLIES and FATIMA HUSSEIN, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Former Tennessee head coach Bill Battle dies at 82
item.description: KNOXVILLE, Tenn. (WATE) -- Former University of Tennessee Coach William "Bill" Battle III died on Thanksgiving morning at the age of 82, according to the university.
item.dc:creator: Hope McAlee, NewsNation

item.title: Amazon worker strike: 5 things to know
item.description: The strike, dubbed "Make Amazon Pay," has planned demonstrations in more than 20 countries including the United States, and the protests are slated to last through Cyber Monday.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Most Americans worried about affording gifts this year
item.description: Most Americans are worried about affording gifts this year but that doesn't mean they're cutting back on spending.
item.dc:creator: Andrew Dorn, NewsNation

item.title: British lawmakers move closer to allowing terminally ill adults to end their lives
item.description: Members of Parliament approved the assisted dying bill by a 330-275 vote, signaling their approval in principle for the bill, which will undergo further scrutiny before it goes to a final vote.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Macy’s Thanksgiving Day Parade: Big balloons, wet weather and 21 protesters arrested
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — The Macy's Thanksgiving Day Parade marched, soared and roared into its second century on Thursday despite a drenching rain and a brief disruption from pro-Palestinian demonstrators.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Returning from a Thanksgiving trip? Watch out for rain, snow
item.description: Travelers who spoke to NewsNation Thursday said the TSA was prepared for the number of passengers coming their way.
item.dc:creator: Xavier Walton, NewsNation

item.title: Stowaway found on Delta flight from New York City to Paris
item.description: The TSA says the woman bypassed two identity verification areas and boarding status stations before getting on the plane.
item.dc:creator: Xavier Walton, NewsNation

item.title: Arizona executions to resume, breaking 2-year pause during review of state death penalty procedures
item.description: PHOENIX (AP) — Executions will resume in Arizona following a two-year pause, the state's top prosecutor says.
item.dc:creator: ANITA SNOW, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Amazon workers on strike from Black Friday to Cyber Monday
item.description: (WFXR) - Amazons workers across 20 countries, including the United States, are striking against what the organizing labor union calls anti-worker and anti-democratic practices.
item.dc:creator: Sydney Jaxtheimer, ABC 4

item.title: Is your turkey dry? This might be why
item.description: Every year in the weeks leading up to Thanksgiving, the holiday conversation inevitably turns to one question regarding the annual feast: is turkey any good? While debates and discussions about which side dishes are the best, it seems like the star of the show often gets overlooked, or worse, malign
item.dc:creator: Travis Schlepp, KRON4

item.title: Why retailers still see Black Friday as the high point of the holiday shopping season
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — After weeks of plugging increasingly inviting discounts, retailers in the United States and several other countries are preparing for prime time: Black Friday, the bargain bonanza that still reigns as the unofficial kickoff of the holiday shopping season even if it's lost some luster
item.dc:creator: ANNE D'INNOCENZIO, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: US faith-based missionaries build homes for Mexican poor south of the border
item.description: A group of 150 faith-based volunteers from North Texas traveled south of the border in the days before Thanksgiving and built several homes for the poor in Reynosa, Mexico. One family sat down with Border Report and detailed their experience.
item.dc:creator: Sandra Sanchez, Fox 5

item.title: Photos: 100 years of the Macy's Thanksgiving Day Parade
item.description: The parade in New York City, presented by department store chain Macy's, was first held in 1924 under the heading "Macy's Christmas Parade" to promote holiday sales and spotlight the newly expanded and, at the time, largest in the world Herald Square store in Manhattan.
item.dc:creator: Nicole Caldwell, Stacker, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: How to avoid starting 'phantom traffic jams' this holiday travel season
item.description: Bumper-to-bumper traffic is a regular occurrence for holiday travelers. However, researchers with Vanderbilt University hope findings from a large traffic study can help.
item.dc:creator: Kori Johnson, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Some of those Ocean Spray cranberries come from a bog in Massachusetts
item.description: MIDDLEBORO, Mass. (AP) — Weeks before Thanksgiving, some of the cranberries that will be on dinner plates Thursday were floating on the Rocky Meadow bog in southeastern Massachusetts. The cranberries have turned this pond pinkish crimson.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL CASEY, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Zach Bryan pauses concert, calls out fan for throwing object on stage
item.description: A country music star whose 'argumentative' behavior has got him in hot water before, paused a recent concert after a fan appears to have thrown something onto stage.
item.dc:creator: Danielle Langenfeld, Fox 5

item.title: Schools are bracing for upheaval over fear of mass deportations
item.description: Last time Donald Trump was president, rumors of immigration raids terrorized the Oregon community where Gustavo Balderas was the school superintendent. Word spread that immigration agents were going to try to enter schools.
item.dc:creator: BIANCA VÁZQUEZ TONESS, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: 3 Americans held for years in China have been released, White House says
item.description: Three American citizens imprisoned for years by China have been released and are returning to the United States, the White House said Wednesday, announcing a rare diplomatic agreement with Beijing in the final months of the Biden administration.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: What dynamic pricing means for Black Friday shopping
item.description: If it feels like prices are on a roller coaster ride during the holidays, you’re not imagining things.
item.dc:creator: Amanda Barroso, Fox 5

item.title: Court rejects request to sideline San Jose State volleyball player on grounds she’s transgender
item.description: A federal appeals court upheld a ruling Tuesday that allows a San Jose State women’s volleyball team member to play in this week’s Mountain West Conference tournament after a legal complaint said she should be ineligible on grounds that she is transgender and thus stronger, posing a safety risk to t
item.dc:creator: AMY BETH HANSON, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Pursuit suspect, police engage in shootout; multiple people detained
item.description: Authorities were pursuing the driver of a fleeing Maserati near West Adams in Los Angeles Tuesday afternoon.
item.dc:creator: Cameron Kiszla, Fox 5

item.title: Dog treats distributed to Florida recalled for potential salmonella
item.description: A chicken-based dog treat sold online and in stores across 19 states has been recalled over the potential risk of salmonella.
item.dc:creator: Carlos Mathis, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Tank explodes, catches fire near GM facility in Michigan
item.description: A tank containing byproducts from oil drilling operations exploded and caught fire Tuesday near the General Motors Proving Ground in southeastern Michigan, damaging two nearby homes but causing no injuries, a fire chief said.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: WATCH: Man throws coffee at charging elk in Colorado
item.description: A video has surfaced of a man throwing what appears to be coffee at a bull elk at Evergreen Lake in Colorado.
item.dc:creator: Greg Nieto, Fox 5

item.title: Drought is causing saltwater to creep up the Delaware River. Here's what's being done about it
item.description: TRENTON, N.J. (AP) — Salty oceanwater is creeping up the Delaware River, the source for much of the drinking water for Philadelphia and millions of others, brought on by drought conditions and sea level rise, and prompting officials to tap reservoirs to push the unpotable tide back downstream.
item.dc:creator: MIKE CATALINI and BRITTANY PETERSON, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Virginia pilot, dog killed in New York plane crash; 2 other dogs found alive
item.description: A Virginia pilot and a rescue dog he was transporting were killed after a plane he was flying crashed in New York on Sunday, authorities said.
item.dc:creator: Jenny Gable, ABC 4

item.title: Photo said to show Amelia Earhart's plane is something else entirely, exploration company says
item.description: “After 11 months the waiting has finally ended and unfortunately our target was not Amelia's Electra 10E," the exploration company said.
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, Fox 5
